lines.english: First, your social status is high.
Second, you are honored in society.
Third, your home is beautiful.
But you are so ugly, with self-conceit in your mind. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਤਿ ਗਰਬੈ ਮੋਹਿ ਫਾਕੀ ਤੂੰ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: at garabai mohi faakee toon |1| rahaau |
lines.english: you have been trapped by your pride and attachment. ||1||Pause||
O beautiful, attractive, wise and clever woman:
Your kitchen is so clean.
You take your bath, and worship, and apply the crimson mark upon your forehead;
with your mouth you speak wisdom, but you are destroyed by pride.
You wear your robes and enjoy pleasures;
you practice good conduct to impress people;
The dog of greed has ruined you in every way. ||2||
you apply scented oils of sandalwood and musk,
but your constant companion is the demon of anger. ||3||
Other people may be your water-carriers;
in this world, you may be a ruler.
but the goodness of your conduct has been destroyed by sexual promiscuity. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਕਉ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਮਇਆ ਹਰਿ ਰਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: jaa kau drisatt meaa har raae |
lines.english: That soul, upon whom the Lord has bestowed His Glance of Grace,
Gold, silver and wealth may be yours,
Joining the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy, the Lord's sublime essence is obtained.
is delivered from bondage.
Says Nanak, how fruitful is that body. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਤਿ ਸੁੰਦਰਿ ਬਿਚਖਨਿ ਤੂੰ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ਦੂਜਾ ॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: at sundar bichakhan toon |1| rahaau doojaa |12|
lines.english: you shall be supremely beautiful and wise. ||1||Second Pause||12||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭਿ ਰੂਪ ਸਭਿ ਸੁਖ ਬਨੇ ਸੁਹਾਗਨਿ ॥
lines.translit: sabh roop sabh sukh bane suhaagan |
lines.english: All graces and all comforts shall come to you, as the happy soul-bride;
Aasaa, Fifth Mehl, Ek-Thukay 2 :
One who is seen to be alive, shall surely die.
But he who is dead shall remain ever-lasting. ||1||
Those who die while yet alive, shall through this death, live on.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਅਵਖਧੁ ਮੁਖਿ ਪਾਇਆ ਗੁਰਸਬਦੀ ਰਸੁ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਪੀਵੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: har har naam avakhadh mukh paaeaa gurasabadee ras amrit peeve |1| rahaau |
lines.english: They place the Name of the Lord, Har, Har, as medicine in their mouths, and through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, they drink in the Ambrosial Nectar. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਛੂਟੈ ਤ੍ਰਿਕੁਟੀ ਤਿਸੁ ਨਿਜ ਘਰਿ ਵਾਸਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jis chhoottai trikuttee tis nij ghar vaasaa |2|
lines.english: One who has eliminated the three qualities dwells in the home of his inner self. ||2||
One who climbs high, shall fall into the nether regions of the underworld.
The clay pot of the body shall be broken.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭ੍ਰਮਤ ਫਿਰੇ ਤਿਨ ਕਿਛੂ ਨ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: bhramat fire tin kichhoo na paaeaa |
lines.english: Those who continue to wander around, achieve nothing.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਰਨਿ ਪੜੈ ਤਿਸੁ ਲਗੈ ਨ ਕਾਲਾ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: dharan parrai tis lagai na kaalaa |3|
lines.english: One who lies upon the ground, shall not be touched by death. ||3||
Those who practice the Guru's Teachings, become steady and stable. ||4||
O Nanak, meeting the Guru, I am enraptured. ||5||13||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਉ ਪਿੰਡੁ ਸਭੁ ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਮਾਲੁ ॥
lines.translit: jeeo pindd sabh har kaa maal |
lines.english: This body and soul all belong to the Lord.
Remember your origins, O thoughtless fool.
Aasaa, Fifth Mehl:
Know for sure that it shall turn to dust. ||1||
The puppet of the body has been fashioned with great skill.
other things are entrusted to you. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਤਨੇ ਕਉ ਤੁਮੑ ਕਿਆ ਗਰਬੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: eitane kau tuma kiaa garabe |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Why are you so proud of yourself? ||1||Pause||
You are a guest, given three meals a day;
you are just excrement, bones and blood, wrapped up in skin
- this is what you are taking such pride in! ||3||
Says Nanak, I am a sacrifice to the Guru;

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕ ਵਸਤੁ ਬੂਝਹਿ ਤਾ ਹੋਵਹਿ ਪਾਕ ॥
lines.translit: ek vasat boojheh taa hoveh paak |
lines.english: If you could understand even one thing, then you would be pure.
Without understanding, you shall be forever impure. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕ ਘੜੀ ਦਿਨਸੁ ਮੋ ਕਉ ਬਹੁਤੁ ਦਿਹਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: eik gharree dinas mo kau bahut dihaare |
lines.english: One moment, one day, is for me many days.
Aasaa, Fifth Mehl, Ek-Thukay, Chau-Padhay:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਤੇ ਪਾਈਐ ਹਰਿ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਸੁਜਾਨੁ ॥੫॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: jis te paaeeai har purakh sujaan |5|14|
lines.english: through Him, I obtain the Lord, the All-knowing Primal Being. ||5||14||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨੁ ਨ ਰਹੈ ਕੈਸੇ ਮਿਲਉ ਪਿਆਰੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: man na rahai kaise milau piaare |1|
lines.english: My mind cannot survive - how can I meet my Beloved? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕੁ ਪਲੁ ਦਿਨਸੁ ਮੋ ਕਉ ਕਬਹੁ ਨ ਬਿਹਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: eik pal dinas mo kau kabahu na bihaavai |
lines.english: I cannot endure one day, even one instant without Him.
